title: European company sells planes to Myanmar junta despite sanctions
subhead: An investigation by Le Monde and the Centre for Information Resilience has uncovered the deliveries of four aircraft from a Czech company to the military dictatorship, despite an EU ban on the export of weapons and goods that could be used for military purposes.

How 'The Camp of the Saints' became the far right's cult novel, from the Le Pens to MAGA
subhead: Since its publication in 1973, Jean Raspail's novel has shaped the imagination of ideologues who oppose immigration. The openly racist story, which envisions a million Indians descending on the French coast, was recently republished across the Atlantic.

title: China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
subhead: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.

title: Willo Perron, the influential designer who stages Rihanna's and Chanel's shows
subhead: The Montreal native first made a name for himself by designing the interiors of American Apparel stores. Since then, this jack-of-all-trades has staged shows for Rihanna, Beyoncé and Drake, and, more recently, directed Chanel's haute couture runway shows at the Grand Palais. He is now returning to his greatest passion, furniture.

title: Looking for Cécile, a 'bad girl' in France's sinister former 'preservation schools'
subhead: Until the mid-20th century, correctional facilities run by the French prison administration held minors accused of theft or 'debauchery' in appalling conditions. Upon his retirement, Michel Bey discovered that his mother, Cécile, who died when he was 5, was one of them.

Fighter jet shot down in Iran, purges within the top ranks: The US military is in turmoil
subhead: Two days after Donald Trump declared that Tehran was nearly defeated, the US was still trying to locate one of the crew members of an F-15E who had to eject over Iran. Meanwhile, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth continued to carry out a series of dismissals, unrelated to the conflict.

title: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
subhead: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.

title: What is Israel's military objective in Lebanon?
subhead: Israel continues to expand its operations, fueling fears of a new occupation of southern Lebanon. While the military's official mission is to completely disarm Hezbollah, its operational goal appears to be simply to weaken the militia-party and create a more or less extensive buffer zone along its border.
